summary: Iteration 2 backend testing complete. All 21 pytest tests passed (100%). Verified new endpoints from this iteration: POST /api/translate/frames (base64 frames -> Gemini ImageContent translation; persists with mode/duration_seconds and returns TranslationItem; 400 on empty frames; default mode applied), POST /api/translate/fingerspelling (returns id/word/letters/detected_language/confidence/notes; persists with mode='fingerspelling'; 400 on empty), GET /api/translation/{id} (returns single item with no _id leak; 404 for missing). Verified GET /api/dictionary returns exactly 68 entries spanning LSE/LSM/ASL. All previously-passing endpoints (health, dictionary filters/languages, text-to-sign, video, history CRUD) still work.

minor.issue: FramesRequest does not enforce a minimum or validate base64 strings; malformed base64 will only fail when reaching Gemini and produce a generic 502. Consider validating inputs (e.g., min_length=1, regex/base64 check) at Pydantic level for clearer 422 responses.
Returns a custom dict (not TranslationItem). The persisted record's translated_text is set to word, but the response shape differs from /frames - inconsistent API surface. Document or unify.
Still returns 200 with deleted:0 instead of 404 when id does not exist (carried over from iteration 1).

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: /api/translate/frames and /api/translate/fingerspelling cap frames at 12 and 14 respectively but never inform the client that frames were truncated. Consider returning a 'frames_used' field for transparency.

server.py: Both new endpoints construct ImageContent(image_base64=f) without any size or count guard. A client could send 14 large base64 images (multi-MB each) per request -- add per-frame max byte length and reject early.
server.py: /api/translate/fingerspelling response is a hand-rolled dict; recommend defining a Pydantic FingerspellResponse model for OpenAPI docs and consistent typing.
server.py: /api/translation/{id} converts created_at strings back to datetime but does not return 404 for soft-malformed records (defensive coding -- low priority).
server.py: SEED_DICTIONARY (68 entries) is now ~75 lines of inline data; consider extracting to backend/data/dictionary.json for maintainability.
server.py: No rate limiting on Gemini-backed endpoints (/translate/video, /frames, /fingerspelling, /text-to-sign). A malicious caller could exhaust the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget.
seed_data_creation: Generated 6 small (64x64) JPEG images in-memory via Pillow and base64-encoded them as the sample_frames pytest fixture for /frames and /fingerspelling tests. Test history entries created during runs are cleaned up by test_clear_history (clears entire collection) and by per-test DELETE calls.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (21 tests, ~90s wall-clock with real Gemini calls). Pillow is available in /root/.venv. Tests import PIL.Image/ImageDraw to synthesize base64 frames. Gemini may return low-confidence on synthetic frames; tests accept either 200 (with structured response and persistence verification via GET /api/translation/{id}) or 502 (Gemini failure) for AI endpoints. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://hands-face-translate.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: No bugs found; all new iteration-2 endpoints behave per spec.
